Ó São Filipe, que a cada dia desconfiáveis de vós mesmo e vos agarráveis à mão de Deus, ensinai-me a conhecer a minha fraqueza sem temor, e a apoiar-me inteiramente na misericórdia de Deus, o único que pode me guardar de cair. Amém.

Intention

For the grace to distrust myself and rely wholly on God.

Pela graça de desconfiar de mim e apoiar-me inteiramente em Deus.

Day 9: Apostle of Rome

Dia 9: Apóstolo de Roma

By a long lifetime of joyful charity, tireless confession, and warm friendship, St. Philip renewed the faith of a whole city and earned the title Apostle of Rome. He labored not by grand schemes but by loving one soul at a time. He shows us that a single cheerful saint, faithful in small and hidden things, can rekindle the love of God in the hearts of many.

Por uma longa vida de caridade alegre, confissão incansável e calorosa amizade, São Filipe renovou a fé de uma cidade inteira e mereceu o título de Apóstolo de Roma. Trabalhou não por grandes planos, mas amando uma alma de cada vez. Ele nos mostra que um único santo alegre, fiel nas coisas pequenas e ocultas, pode reacender o amor de Deus no coração de muitos.

Prayer

O glorious St. Philip Neri, Apostle of Rome, who renewed a city by loving souls one by one, present to God the petition I now entrust to you, and obtain for me, with your holy joy, the grace I most desire. Amen.

Ó glorioso São Filipe Néri, Apóstolo de Roma, que renovastes uma cidade amando as almas uma a uma, apresentai a Deus a súplica que agora vos confio, e alcançai-me, com a vossa santa alegria, a graça que mais desejo. Amém.

Intention

For the particular grace I now entrust to St. Philip Neri.

Pela graça particular que agora confio a São Filipe Néri.

Closing prayer

O St. Philip, whose heart was enlarged by the Holy Spirit, present my petitions to the Lord you loved so dearly. Teach me to delight in God and to lead others to Him by kindness rather than by harshness, until we meet in the joy of heaven. Amen.

Ó São Filipe, cujo coração foi dilatado pelo Espírito Santo, apresentai os meus pedidos ao Senhor que tanto amastes. Ensinai-me a deleitar-me em Deus e a conduzir os outros a Ele pela bondade e não pela dureza, até nos encontrarmos na alegria do céu. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="neri">
Novena
to St. Philip Neri

This novena comes from the Oratorians in Toronto, who
say it has been adapted from the prayers and devotions of Cardinal
Newman. It is prayed especially beginning on May 17 and ending on May
25, the eve of the Feast of St. Philip Neri

Day One:

Philip, my glorious Patron, who didst count as dross the praise, and
even the good esteem of men, obtain for me also, from my Lord and
Savior, this fair virtue by thy prayers. How haughty are my thoughts,
how contemptuous are my words, how ambitious are my works. Gain for me
that low esteem of self with which thou wast gifted; obtain for me a
knowledge of my own nothingness, that I may rejoice when I am despised,
and ever seek to be great only in the eyes of my God and Judge. Through
Christ Our Lord. Amen.

Day Two:

Philip, my glorious Patron, gain for me a portion of that gift which
thou hadst so abundantly. Alas! thy heart was burning with love; mine
is all frozen towards God, and alive only for creatures. I love the
world, which can never make me happy; my highest desire is to be well
off here below. O my God, when shall I learn to love nothing else but
Thee? Gain for me, O Philip, a pure love, a strong love, and an
efficacious love, that, loving God here upon earth, I may enjoy the
sight of Him together with thee and all the saints, hereafter in
Heaven. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.

Day Three:

Philip, my holy Patron, teach me by thy example, and gain for me by thy
intercessions, to seek my Lord and God at all times and in all places,
and to live in His presence and in sacred intercourse with Him. As the
children of this world look up to rich men or men in station for the
favor which they desire, so may I ever lift up my eyes and hands and
heart towards heaven, and betake myself to the source of all good for
those goods which I need. As the children of this world converse with
their friends and find their pleasure in them, so may I ever hold
communion with Saints and Angels, and with the Blessed Virgin, the
Mother of my Lord. Pray with me, O Philip, as thou didst pray with thy
penitents here below, and then prayer will become sweet to me as it did
to them. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.

Day Four:

Philip, my glorious Patron, who didst ever keep unsullied the white
lily of thy purity, with such jealous care that the majesty of this
fair virtue beamed from thine eyes, shone in thy hands, and was
fragrant in thy breath, obtain for me that gift from the Holy Ghost,
that neither the words nor the example of sinners may ever make any
impression on my soul. And, since it is by avoiding occasions of sin,
by prayer, by keeping myself employed, and by frequent use of the
Sacraments that my dread enemy must be subdued, gain for me the grace
to persevere in these necessary observances. Through Christ our Lord.
Amen.

Day Five:

Philip, my glorious Advocate, teach me to look at all I see around me
after thy pattern as the creatures of God. Let me never forget that the
same God who made me made the whole world, and all men and all animals
that are in it. Gain for me the grace to love all God's works for His
sake, and all men for the sake of my Lord and Savior who has redeemed
them by the Cross. And especially let me be tender and compassionate
and loving towards all Christians, as my brethren in grace. And do
thou, who on earth wast so tender to all, be especially tender to us,
and feel for us, bear with us in all our troubles, and gain for us from
God, with whom thou dwellest in beatific light, all the aids necessary
for bringing us safely to Him and to thee. Through Christ our Lord.
Amen.

Day Six:

Philip, my glorious Advocate, who didst ever follow the precepts and
example of the Apostle Saint Paul in rejoicing always in all things,
gain for me the grace of perfect resignation to God's will, of
indifference to matters of this world, and a constant sight of Heaven;
so that I may never

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas reproduces the classic nine-day St. Philip Neri novena (https://www.praymorenovenas.com/st-philip-neri-novena), Trinitarian blessing opening and closing, with a unique daily prayer beginning "Philip, my glorious patron/advocate..." Daily themes: Day 1 humility; Day 2 devotion / divine love; Day 3 the exercise of prayer; Day 4 purity; Day 5 tenderness of heart; Day 6 cheerfulness; Day 7 patience; Day 8 care for the salvation of souls; Day 9 miraculous gifts.

Fidelity note

Ember's prayers are an original composition and do not reuse the "Philip, my glorious patron..." petitions of the classic Newman/PrayMoreNovenas text. The two overlap clearly on the saint's hallmark themes — joy/cheerfulness, humility, prayer, purity (Ember folds this into the wider "enflamed heart"/Spirit material), tenderness of heart ("cor ad cor loquitur"), and zeal for souls. Ember adds well-attested biographical days the source treats only lightly (the streets of Rome, the catacombs/enlarged heart, the Oratory, "Apostle of Rome"). Faithful to St. Philip's spirit and the traditional themes, but freshly worded.

St. José de Anchieta st-jose-de-anchieta-novena

May 31–Jun 8
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. José de Anchieta, the Jesuit missionary known as the Apostle of Brazil.

Uma novena de nove dias a São José de Anchieta, o missionário jesuíta conhecido como o Apóstolo do Brasil.

St. José de Anchieta (1534–1597), born in Tenerife, entered the Society of Jesus and sailed to Brazil in 1553. With Manuel da Nóbrega he helped found the mission of São Paulo de Piratininga and later Rio de Janeiro, learning the Tupi language and writing its first grammar. A poet and tireless catechist of the indigenous peoples, he was canonized by Pope Francis in 2014.

São José de Anchieta (1534–1597), nascido em Tenerife, entrou na Companhia de Jesus e partiu para o Brasil em 1553. Com Manuel da Nóbrega ajudou a fundar a missão de São Paulo de Piratininga e, mais tarde, o Rio de Janeiro, aprendendo a língua tupi e escrevendo sua primeira gramática. Poeta e incansável catequista dos povos indígenas, foi canonizado pelo Papa Francisco em 2014.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O holy José de Anchieta, missionary who carried Christ across the seas to a new land, look upon me as I begin this novena. Win for me your zeal for souls and your tender love for Our Lady, that I may labor for God wherever He sends me. Amen.

Ó santo José de Anchieta, missionário que levastes Cristo além dos mares a uma terra nova, olhai para mim ao iniciar esta novena. Alcançai-me o vosso zelo pelas almas e o vosso terno amor a Nossa Senhora, para que eu trabalhe por Deus onde quer que Ele me envie. Amém.

Day 1: The call across the sea

Dia 1: O chamado além-mar

Still a young Jesuit of nineteen, José de Anchieta left his native Canary Islands and crossed the wide Atlantic in 1553 toward a land he had never seen, trusting wholly in God. Frail in body, he gave himself without reserve to the unknown. He teaches us that the Lord's call may lead far from home and all that is familiar, and that love makes even the hardest journey light.

Ainda jovem jesuíta de dezenove anos, José de Anchieta deixou suas Ilhas Canárias natais e cruzou o vasto Atlântico em 1553 rumo a uma terra que nunca vira, confiando inteiramente em Deus. Frágil de corpo, entregou-se sem reservas ao desconhecido. Ele nos ensina que o chamado do Senhor pode levar para longe de casa e de tudo o que é familiar, e que o amor torna leve até a mais dura jornada.

Prayer

O St. José de Anchieta, who crossed the sea at God's call into an unknown land, give me your trusting courage, that I may follow the Lord wherever He leads, leaving behind whatever He asks for the sake of His Gospel. Amen.

Ó São José de Anchieta, que cruzastes o mar ao chamado de Deus rumo a uma terra desconhecida, dai-me a vossa coragem confiante, para que eu siga o Senhor aonde quer que Ele conduza, deixando para trás tudo o que Ele pedir por amor do Seu Evangelho. Amém.

Intention

For courage to follow God's call wherever it leads.

Pela coragem de seguir o chamado de Deus aonde quer que ele conduza.

Day 2: Founder of São Paulo

Dia 2: Fundador de São Paulo

In 1554 Anchieta and his companions raised the humble mission school of São Paulo de Piratininga on a high plain, and on its feast the very name of the future city was given. From a poor hut of mud and straw he taught and prayed, laying a spiritual foundation that would outlast empires. He shows us that the greatest works of God often begin in poverty and obscurity.

Em 1554 Anchieta e seus companheiros ergueram o humilde colégio missionário de São Paulo de Piratininga sobre um planalto, e na festa do dia o próprio nome da futura cidade foi dado. De uma pobre choça de barro e palha ele ensinava e orava, lançando um alicerce espiritual que sobreviveria a impérios. Ele nos mostra que as maiores obras de Deus muitas vezes começam na pobreza e na obscuridade.

Prayer

O St. José de Anchieta, who built the Church in a poor hut on a distant plain, bless all who labor for the faith in forgotten and humble places, and teach me to value the hidden beginnings from which God raises great things. Amen.

Ó São José de Anchieta, que edificastes a Igreja numa pobre choça sobre um planalto distante, abençoai todos os que trabalham pela fé em lugares esquecidos e humildes, e ensinai-me a valorizar os começos ocultos a partir dos quais Deus suscita grandes coisas. Amém.

Intention

For all who build the Church in poor and forgotten places.

Por todos os que edificam a Igreja em lugares pobres e esquecidos.

Day 3: Learning the people's tongue

Dia 3: Aprender a língua do povo

Patiently Anchieta mastered the Tupi language and wrote its first grammar, so that the Gospel and the prayers of the Church might be preached and sung in the people's own words. He composed catechisms and hymns in their tongue. He teaches us that true love takes the trouble to understand others as they are, meeting them in their own language and their own world.

Pacientemente Anchieta dominou a língua tupi e escreveu sua primeira gramática, para que o Evangelho e as orações da Igreja pudessem ser pregados e cantados nas próprias palavras do povo. Compôs catecismos e hinos no idioma deles. Ele nos ensina que o verdadeiro amor se dá ao trabalho de compreender os outros como eles são, indo ao seu encontro em sua própria língua e em seu próprio mundo.

Prayer

O St. José de Anchieta, who learned a foreign tongue to bring the Gospel to others, give me a love patient enough to truly understand my neighbor, and bless all missionaries who labor to share the faith in every land and culture. Amen.

Ó São José de Anchieta, que aprendestes uma língua estrangeira para levar o Evangelho aos outros, dai-me um amor paciente o bastante para verdadeiramente compreender o meu próximo, e abençoai todos os missionários que se esforçam por partilhar a fé em toda terra e cultura. Amém.

Intention

For missionaries who labor to share the faith in every culture.

Pelos missionários que se esforçam por partilhar a fé em toda cultura.

Day 4: The poem in the sand

Dia 4: O poema na areia

Held for months as a hostage among the Tamoio at Iperoig, in daily danger of death, Anchieta composed a long Latin poem of more than four thousand verses in praise of the Blessed Virgin, writing them each morning on the wet sand of the shore and committing them to memory. He shows us that devotion to Mary can flower even in captivity and fear, turning a prison into a place of song.

Mantido por meses como refém entre os tamoios em Iperoig, em perigo diário de morte, Anchieta compôs um longo poema latino de mais de quatro mil versos em louvor à Virgem Maria, escrevendo-os a cada manhã na areia molhada da praia e guardando-os de memória. Ele nos mostra que a devoção a Maria pode florescer mesmo no cativeiro e no medo, transformando uma prisão em lugar de canto.

Prayer

O St. José de Anchieta, who sang the praises of Our Lady even in captivity and fear, deepen in me a constant love for the Mother of God, that in every trial I too may turn my heart to her and find peace. Amen.

Ó São José de Anchieta, que cantastes os louvores de Nossa Senhora mesmo no cativeiro e no medo, aprofundai em mim um amor constante à Mãe de Deus, para que em toda provação também eu volte o coração para ela e encontre a paz. Amém.

Intention

For a deeper and more constant love for Our Lady.

Por um amor mais profundo e constante a Nossa Senhora.

Day 5: Peacemaker among nations

Dia 5: Pacificador entre os povos

During the long conflict with the Tamoio, Anchieta offered himself as a hostage and risked his life to broker the peace of Iperoig, standing unarmed between warring peoples for the safety of others. He sought not to conquer but to reconcile. He teaches us that the servant of Christ is sent to be a peacemaker, who, blessed by the Lord, lays down his own safety for the good of all.

Durante o longo conflito com os tamoios, Anchieta ofereceu-se como refém e arriscou a vida para mediar a paz de Iperoig, pondo-se desarmado entre povos em guerra pela segurança dos demais. Buscava não conquistar, mas reconciliar. Ele nos ensina que o servo de Cristo é enviado para ser pacificador, que, abençoado pelo Senhor, depõe a própria segurança pelo bem de todos.

Prayer

O St. José de Anchieta, who risked your life to make peace between warring peoples, make me an instrument of reconciliation in every quarrel, and bring God's peace to all nations and hearts divided by enmity and fear. Amen.

Ó São José de Anchieta, que arriscastes a vida para fazer a paz entre povos em guerra, fazei de mim um instrumento de reconciliação em toda discórdia, e levai a paz de Deus a todos os povos e corações divididos pela inimizade e pelo medo. Amém.

Intention

For peace among peoples divided by enmity.

Pela paz entre os povos divididos pela inimizade.

Day 6: Healer and comforter

Dia 6: Curador e consolador

Anchieta tended the sick with his own hands, dressing wounds and nursing the dying, and many wonders of healing were credited to his prayers among the native peoples and colonists alike. He saw Christ in every sufferer. He reminds us that the love of God is shown as much in tender care for the body as in care for the soul, and that mercy heals what medicine cannot.

Anchieta cuidava dos doentes com as próprias mãos, tratando feridas e amparando os moribundos, e muitos prodígios de cura foram atribuídos às suas orações, tanto entre os indígenas quanto entre os colonos. Via Cristo em cada sofredor. Ele nos recorda que o amor de Deus se manifesta tanto no terno cuidado do corpo quanto no cuidado da alma, e que a misericórdia cura o que a medicina não alcança.

Prayer

O St. José de Anchieta, who healed the sick and comforted the dying in the name of Christ, look with mercy on all who suffer in body or soul, and strengthen the hands of those who care for them. Amen.

Ó São José de Anchieta, que curastes os doentes e consolastes os moribundos em nome de Cristo, olhai com misericórdia para todos os que sofrem no corpo ou na alma, e fortalecei as mãos dos que deles cuidam. Amém.

Intention

For the sick and for those who care for them.

Pelos doentes e por aqueles que deles cuidam.

Day 7: Teacher and dramatist

Dia 7: Mestre e dramaturgo

To teach the faith and delight his people, Anchieta wrote songs, catechisms, and sacred plays performed in Tupi, Portuguese, and Spanish, becoming the first dramatist of Brazil. He pressed poetry and theater into the service of the Gospel. He shows us that the truths of God may be taught with beauty, and that every human gift and art can be offered wholly to the Lord's service.

Para ensinar a fé e alegrar o seu povo, Anchieta escreveu cânticos, catecismos e autos sacros encenados em tupi, português e espanhol, tornando-se o primeiro dramaturgo do Brasil. Pôs a poesia e o teatro a serviço do Evangelho. Ele nos mostra que as verdades de Deus podem ser ensinadas com beleza, e que todo dom e arte humanos podem ser oferecidos inteiramente ao serviço do Senhor.

Prayer

O St. José de Anchieta, poet and teacher who made beauty serve the Gospel, bless all teachers and artists, and help me to offer my own gifts, however small, wholly to the glory of God and the good of others. Amen.

Ó São José de Anchieta, poeta e mestre que fizestes a beleza servir ao Evangelho, abençoai todos os professores e artistas, e ajudai-me a oferecer os meus próprios dons, por menores que sejam, inteiramente à glória de Deus e ao bem dos outros. Amém.

Intention

For teachers and for all who serve God through their gifts.

Pelos professores e por todos os que servem a Deus com seus dons.

Day 8: A life poured out

Dia 8: Uma vida derramada

For more than forty years Anchieta walked thousands of miles through forest and along the coast, often barefoot, ailing, and bent with infirmity, never sparing himself for the souls entrusted to him. He spent his strength like a candle burning down. He teaches us that holiness is not a single great deed but the patient gift of self, offered day after day until nothing is held back.

Por mais de quarenta anos Anchieta percorreu milhares de léguas por matas e pela costa, muitas vezes descalço, enfermo e curvado pela doença, sem nunca se poupar pelas almas a ele confiadas. Gastou as suas forças como uma vela que se consome. Ele nos ensina que a santidade não é um único grande feito, mas a doação paciente de si, oferecida dia após dia até que nada se reserve.

Prayer

O St. José de Anchieta, who spent your whole strength for the souls God gave you, obtain for me the grace to serve others faithfully without counting the cost, persevering in love until my last day. Amen.

Ó São José de Anchieta, que gastastes todas as vossas forças pelas almas que Deus vos deu, alcançai-me a graça de servir os outros com fidelidade sem medir o custo, perseverando no amor até o meu último dia. Amém.

Intention

For perseverance in serving others without counting the cost.

Pela perseverança em servir os outros sem medir o custo.

Day 9: Apostle of Brazil

Dia 9: Apóstolo do Brasil

When he died in 1597 the people wept for the loss of a father, carrying his body in procession amid the lament of the very tribes he had served. The Church, having canonized him in 2014, now honors him as the Apostle of Brazil. He assures us that a life given wholly to God is never wasted, but leaves a harvest of faith and love that endures for centuries.

Quando morreu, em 1597, o povo chorou a perda de um pai, carregando o seu corpo em procissão em meio ao pranto das próprias tribos que servira. A Igreja, tendo-o canonizado em 2014, agora o honra como o Apóstolo do Brasil. Ele nos assegura que uma vida inteiramente entregue a Deus nunca é desperdiçada, mas deixa uma colheita de fé e amor que perdura por séculos.

Prayer

O glorious St. José de Anchieta, Apostle of Brazil, who gave your whole life to God and left a harvest of faith, present to Him the petition I now entrust to you, and obtain for me the grace I most desire. Amen.

Ó glorioso São José de Anchieta, Apóstolo do Brasil, que entregastes a vossa vida inteira a Deus e deixastes uma colheita de fé, apresentai-Lhe a súplica que agora vos confio, e alcançai-me a graça que mais desejo. Amém.

Intention

For the particular grace I now entrust to St. José de Anchieta.

Pela graça particular que agora confio a São José de Anchieta.

Closing prayer

O St. José de Anchieta, Apostle of Brazil, present my petitions to the Lord for whom you spent your whole life. Teach me to love those of every tongue and people, and to seek God's glory above my own comfort, until I praise Him with you forever. Amen.

Ó São José de Anchieta, Apóstolo do Brasil, apresentai os meus pedidos ao Senhor por quem gastastes toda a vossa vida. Ensinai-me a amar os homens de toda língua e povo e a buscar a glória de Deus acima do meu conforto, até louvá-Lo convosco para sempre. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

—
Fidelity note

St. José de Anchieta (the Jesuit "Apostle of Brazil," canonized 2014) has no English-language novena on FishEaters or PrayMoreNovenas, so there is no comparison text. Ember's novena is an original composition that hews closely to the well-documented facts of his life: the voyage from the Canary Islands, the 1554 founding of São Paulo de Piratininga, his Tupi grammar, the Marian Latin poem composed on the sand during his Tamoio captivity, his peacemaking, healing, sacred plays, and the title Apostle of Brazil. The biography is accurate and the themes are devotionally apt; as an original work there is nothing to diverge from.

St. Aloysius Gonzaga st-aloysius-gonzaga-novena

Jun 12–20
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Aloysius Gonzaga, the angelic Jesuit youth and patron of the young.

Uma novena de nove dias a São Luís Gonzaga, o angélico jovem jesuíta e padroeiro da juventude.

St. Aloysius Gonzaga (1568–1591) was born to a noble Italian family destined for the court, yet from boyhood he gave himself wholly to God in prayer and purity. Renouncing his inheritance, he entered the Society of Jesus over his father's resistance. While caring for the sick in a plague hospital in Rome, he contracted the disease and died at twenty-three; he is the patron of youth and students.

São Luís Gonzaga (1568–1591) nasceu numa nobre família italiana destinada à corte, mas desde menino entregou-se inteiramente a Deus na oração e na pureza. Renunciando à herança, entrou na Companhia de Jesus apesar da resistência do pai. Cuidando dos doentes num hospital de peste em Roma, contraiu a doença e morreu aos vinte e três anos; é o padroeiro da juventude e dos estudantes.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O angelic St. Aloysius, who in your youth loved God with an undivided heart, look upon me as I begin this novena. Win for me purity of mind and body, and a love of God that nothing in this world can dim. Amen.

Ó angélico São Luís, que na juventude amastes a Deus com coração indiviso, olhai para mim ao iniciar esta novena. Alcançai-me pureza de mente e de corpo e um amor de Deus que nada neste mundo possa apagar. Amém.

Day 1: Early love of God

Dia 1: Amor precoce a Deus

While still a small child Aloysius began to seek God, finding delight in prayer and the recitation of the psalms, and at the age of nine he made a private vow of perpetual chastity before Our Lady's altar in Florence. Grace ran ahead of his years. He teaches us that the young heart is never too small or too inexperienced to give itself wholly and forever to the Lord.

Ainda criança pequena, Luís começou a buscar a Deus, encontrando deleite na oração e no recitar dos salmos, e aos nove anos fez voto particular de castidade perpétua diante do altar de Nossa Senhora em Florença. A graça correu adiante dos seus anos. Ele nos ensina que o coração jovem nunca é pequeno demais nem inexperiente demais para se entregar inteira e perpetuamente ao Senhor.

Prayer

O St. Aloysius, who gave your heart to God in earliest childhood, watch over all the young, that they may come to know and love the Lord from their first years, and offer Him the fresh and undivided gift of their youth. Amen.

Ó São Luís, que destes o vosso coração a Deus na mais tenra infância, velai por todos os jovens, para que cheguem a conhecer e amar o Senhor desde os primeiros anos, e Lhe ofereçam o dom fresco e indiviso da sua juventude. Amém.

Intention

For all children, that they may know and love God early.

Por todas as crianças, para que conheçam e amem a Deus desde cedo.

Day 2: Purity of heart

Dia 2: Pureza de coração

Aloysius guarded his eyes and senses so faithfully, even at the worldly courts where he was raised, that it was said he never looked his own mother full in the face, and his innocence shone like that of an angel. His purity was no fearful coldness but a fierce, loving custody of the heart. He shows us that purity keeps the soul undivided and wholly free for the love of God.

Luís guardava os olhos e os sentidos com tanta fidelidade, mesmo nas cortes mundanas onde foi criado, que se dizia nunca ter fitado o rosto da própria mãe, e sua inocência brilhava como a de um anjo. Sua pureza não era uma frieza temerosa, mas uma firme e amorosa custódia do coração. Ele nos mostra que a pureza mantém a alma indivisa e inteiramente livre para o amor de Deus.

Prayer

O angelic St. Aloysius, lover of holy purity, obtain for me a clean heart and watchful senses, that I may guard my soul undivided for God alone, and behold Him one day with the pure of heart. Amen.

Ó angélico São Luís, amante da santa pureza, alcançai-me um coração limpo e sentidos vigilantes, para que eu guarde a minha alma indivisa só para Deus, e um dia O contemple com os puros de coração. Amém.

Intention

For the grace of purity in thought, word, and deed.

Pela graça da pureza no pensamento, na palavra e na ação.

Day 3: Renouncing nobility

Dia 3: Renúncia à nobreza

Eldest son and heir to the Marquisate of Castiglione, Aloysius signed away his title, lands, and inheritance to his younger brother that he might follow Christ in the poverty of the Society of Jesus. He counted rank and riches as loss for the surpassing worth of Christ. He teaches us that no estate or honor on earth is worth purchasing at the price of the kingdom of heaven.

Filho primogênito e herdeiro do Marquesado de Castiglione, Luís abriu mão do título, das terras e da herança em favor do irmão mais novo, para seguir Cristo na pobreza da Companhia de Jesus. Considerou posição e riquezas como perda diante do valor superior de Cristo. Ele nos ensina que nenhum patrimônio ou honra na terra vale ser comprado ao preço do reino dos céus.

Prayer

O St. Aloysius, who renounced rank and riches for the love of Christ, free my heart from greed and worldly ambition, that I may treasure the kingdom of heaven above every earthly gain, and follow the Lord in holy poverty of spirit. Amen.

Ó São Luís, que renunciastes à posição e às riquezas por amor a Cristo, libertai o meu coração da cobiça e da ambição mundana, para que eu tenha por tesouro o reino dos céus acima de todo ganho terreno, e siga o Senhor na santa pobreza de espírito. Amém.

Intention

For detachment from riches and worldly ambition.

Pelo desapego das riquezas e da ambição mundana.

Day 4: Obedience over his father's will

Dia 4: Obediência apesar da vontade do pai

For three long years Aloysius patiently bore his father's anger, pleadings, and schemes to keep him from the Jesuits, never once losing his temper nor yielding his resolve. He answered every pressure with gentle, unshakable love. He shows us that we may truly honor our parents and still obey God's higher claim, holding firm to a holy vocation with patience rather than defiance.

Por três longos anos Luís suportou com paciência a ira, as súplicas e as artimanhas do pai para afastá-lo dos jesuítas, sem jamais perder a calma nem ceder em seu propósito. Respondia a toda pressão com amor manso e inabalável. Ele nos mostra que podemos honrar verdadeiramente os pais e ainda assim obedecer ao chamado superior de Deus, mantendo firme uma santa vocação com paciência, e não com rebeldia.

Prayer

O St. Aloysius, who held fast to your vocation with patient love despite your father's resistance, strengthen all young people seeking to follow God's call, that they may honor their parents and yet remain faithful to His higher will. Amen.

Ó São Luís, que vos mantivestes firme na vossa vocação com amor paciente, apesar da resistência do pai, fortalecei todos os jovens que buscam seguir o chamado de Deus, para que honrem os pais e, ainda assim, permaneçam fiéis à Sua vontade superior. Amém.

Intention

For young people seeking to follow their vocation faithfully.

Pelos jovens que buscam seguir fielmente a sua vocação.

Day 5: A life of penance

Dia 5: Uma vida de penitência

Aloysius embraced fasting, long hours of prayer, and hard mortification with such ardor that his superiors had to restrain his fervor and command him to spare his health. His love would give and give, never spare itself. He reminds us that genuine love for God presses the soul not to comfort but to generous self-denial, while true obedience even moderates penance at a superior's word.

Luís abraçou o jejum, as longas horas de oração e a mortificação severa com tal ardor que seus superiores tiveram de moderar o seu fervor e ordenar-lhe que poupasse a saúde. Seu amor queria dar e dar, sem nunca se poupar. Ele nos recorda que o amor genuíno a Deus impele a alma não ao conforto, mas à generosa renúncia, ao passo que a verdadeira obediência modera até a penitência à palavra do superior.

Prayer

O St. Aloysius, generous in penance and humble in obedience, give me the grace to deny myself for love of God, and to offer my small sacrifices with a willing heart, ever guided by holy obedience. Amen.

Ó São Luís, generoso na penitência e humilde na obediência, dai-me a graça de renunciar a mim mesmo por amor a Deus, e de oferecer os meus pequenos sacrifícios com coração disposto, sempre guiado pela santa obediência. Amém.

Intention

For the grace to deny myself for love of God.

Pela graça de renunciar a mim mesmo por amor a Deus.

Day 6: Devotion to the Eucharist

Dia 6: Devoção à Eucaristia

So great was the love of Aloysius for the Blessed Sacrament that he prepared for Holy Communion with three days of longing and gave thanks afterward for three days more, often rapt in ecstasy before the tabernacle. The altar was the center of his whole life. He teaches us to approach the Eucharist with deep reverence, burning desire, and a heart made ready by prayer.

Tão grande era o amor de Luís pelo Santíssimo Sacramento que se preparava para a Sagrada Comunhão com três dias de desejo e dava graças por mais três dias depois, muitas vezes arrebatado em êxtase diante do sacrário. O altar era o centro de toda a sua vida. Ele nos ensina a aproximar-nos da Eucaristia com profunda reverência, ardente desejo e um coração preparado pela oração.

Prayer

O St. Aloysius, who loved the Holy Eucharist with a burning heart, deepen my reverence and longing for the Blessed Sacrament, that each Communion may unite me more closely to Christ, the bread of life. Amen.

Ó São Luís, que amastes a Santa Eucaristia com um coração ardente, aprofundai a minha reverência e o meu desejo pelo Santíssimo Sacramento, para que cada Comunhão me una mais estreitamente a Cristo, o pão da vida. Amém.

Intention

For a deeper reverence and love for the Holy Eucharist.

Por uma reverência e um amor mais profundos à Santa Eucaristia.

Day 7: Serving the plague-stricken

Dia 7: Servir os atingidos pela peste

When plague swept through Rome in 1591, Aloysius begged leave to nurse the dying, carrying the stricken on his own shoulders to the hospital, washing them, and tending their last needs with his noble hands. He feared no contagion where charity called. He shows us that genuine love of neighbor counts no danger too great, and lays down its own safety for the sake of the suffering.

Quando a peste assolou Roma em 1591, Luís suplicou licença para cuidar dos moribundos, carregando os atingidos nos próprios ombros até o hospital, lavando-os e atendendo às suas últimas necessidades com suas mãos nobres. Não temia contágio algum onde a caridade o chamava. Ele nos mostra que o verdadeiro amor ao próximo não conta perigo algum como grande demais, e depõe a própria segurança pelo bem dos que sofrem.

Prayer

O St. Aloysius, who served the plague-stricken without fear for yourself, inflame my heart with generous charity toward the sick and the suffering, that I may see and serve Christ in every neighbor in need. Amen.

Ó São Luís, que servistes os atingidos pela peste sem temor por vós mesmo, inflamai o meu coração com generosa caridade para com os doentes e os que sofrem, para que eu veja e sirva Cristo em todo próximo necessitado. Amém.

Intention

For generous service to the sick and the suffering.

Pelo serviço generoso aos doentes e aos que sofrem.

Day 8: Joyful acceptance of death

Dia 8: Aceitação alegre da morte

Stricken by the very plague he had nursed, Aloysius welcomed his approaching death with serene joy, telling his friends he was 'going gladly' to God. He died in the night, the name of Jesus on his lips, at only twenty-three. He teaches us to meet our last hour without fear, seeing death not as an enemy but as the gateway to the One we have loved and longed for.

Atingido pela mesma peste que tratara, Luís acolheu a sua morte próxima com serena alegria, dizendo aos amigos que partia 'com alegria' para Deus. Morreu de noite, com o nome de Jesus nos lábios, aos vinte e três anos apenas. Ele nos ensina a enfrentar a última hora sem medo, vendo a morte não como inimiga, mas como a porta para Aquele que amamos e desejamos.

Prayer

O St. Aloysius, who welcomed death with joy as the gateway to God, obtain for me the grace of a holy and peaceful death, that I may meet my last hour without fear, longing only to be united forever with the Lord. Amen.

Ó São Luís, que acolhestes a morte com alegria como a porta para Deus, alcançai-me a graça de uma morte santa e serena, para que eu enfrente a minha última hora sem medo, desejando apenas unir-me para sempre ao Senhor. Amém.

Intention

For the grace of a holy and peaceful death.

Pela graça de uma morte santa e serena.

Day 9: Patron of youth

Dia 9: Padroeiro da juventude

In only twenty-three years Aloysius reached a height of holiness that the Church now holds up before every young person as a model and an intercessor. Declared patron of youth and of students, he proves that sanctity belongs to the young. He assures us that holiness is measured not by length of days but by the fullness of love poured out for God and neighbor.

Em apenas vinte e três anos Luís alcançou um grau de santidade que a Igreja hoje propõe a todo jovem como modelo e intercessor. Declarado padroeiro da juventude e dos estudantes, prova que a santidade pertence aos jovens. Ele nos assegura que a santidade não se mede pela duração dos dias, mas pela plenitude do amor derramado por Deus e pelo próximo.

Prayer

O glorious St. Aloysius Gonzaga, patron of youth, who reached great holiness in few years, present to God the petition I now entrust to you, and obtain for me, and for all the young, the grace I most desire. Amen.

Ó glorioso São Luís Gonzaga, padroeiro da juventude, que alcançastes grande santidade em poucos anos, apresentai a Deus a súplica que agora vos confio, e alcançai-me, e a toda a juventude, a graça que mais desejo. Amém.

Intention

For the particular grace I now entrust to St. Aloysius.

Pela graça particular que agora confio a São Luís Gonzaga.

Closing prayer

O St. Aloysius Gonzaga, patron of the young, present my petitions to the Lord you served so faithfully. Help me to flee what offends God and to spend my strength in His service, that with you I may one day behold His face. Amen.

Ó São Luís Gonzaga, padroeiro da juventude, apresentai os meus pedidos ao Senhor que servistes com tanta fidelidade. Ajudai-me a fugir do que ofende a Deus e a gastar minhas forças em Seu serviço, para que convosco eu contemple um dia o Seu rosto. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

— not on FishEaters / not retrieved

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas has a St. Aloysius Gonzaga novena (https://www.praymorenovenas.com/st-aloysius-gonzaga-novena). Each day opens with a Trinitarian blessing and a thanksgiving for St. Aloysius as an example, gives a daily meditation on his life and a "Pray for me..." petition, space for personal intentions, and closes "St. Aloysius Gonzaga, pray for us!" with a Trinitarian blessing. Daily themes: Day 1 beginning holiness in youth (his turn to prayer in illness); Day 2 discerning God's will (drawn by Jesuit missionaries); Day 3 radical religious commitment despite family opposition; Day 4 renouncing his worldly inheritance; Day 5 persevering through opposition; Day 6 service through personal suffering / chronic illness; Day 7 courage in caring for plague victims; Day 8 patient endurance of suffering; Day 9 faithful service unto death.

Fidelity note

Ember's text is original and shares no wording with PrayMoreNovenas, but the two cover the same biography of the young Jesuit and overlap heavily in theme: youthful holiness, renouncing nobility, persevering against family opposition, serving the plague-stricken, and a holy early death. Ember adds emphases the source treats only lightly — purity/chastity (his vow at nine), penance, and Eucharistic devotion — which are central to Aloysius's traditional cult, so they strengthen rather than distort the fidelity. A faithful, well-grounded original composition closely parallel to the source in structure and substance.

St. Thomas More st-thomas-more-novena

Jun 13–21
description & history

A nine-day novena to St. Thomas More, martyr for conscience and patron of statesmen and lawyers.

Uma novena de nove dias a São Tomás Moro, mártir da consciência e padroeiro dos estadistas e advogados.

St. Thomas More (1478–1535) was a brilliant lawyer, scholar, and devoted family man who rose to become Lord Chancellor of England under Henry VIII. When the king demanded he approve the royal supremacy over the Church, More refused, resigned his office, and was imprisoned in the Tower. He went to the scaffold with serene humor, declaring himself 'the King's good servant, but God's first,' and was beheaded for his fidelity to conscience and the Church.

São Tomás Moro (1478–1535) foi um brilhante advogado, erudito e dedicado pai de família que chegou a Lorde Chanceler da Inglaterra sob Henrique VIII. Quando o rei exigiu que aprovasse a supremacia real sobre a Igreja, Moro recusou, renunciou ao cargo e foi preso na Torre. Subiu ao cadafalso com serenidade e bom humor, declarando-se 'o bom servo do rei, mas primeiro de Deus', e foi decapitado por sua fidelidade à consciência e à Igreja.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

O steadfast St. Thomas More, who held fast to God's law amid the favor and threats of kings, look upon me as I begin this novena. Win for me a clear conscience and the courage to keep it, whatever the cost. Amen.

Ó firme São Tomás Moro, que vos mantivestes fiel à lei de Deus em meio ao favor e às ameaças dos reis, olhai para mim ao iniciar esta novena. Alcançai-me uma consciência reta e a coragem de mantê-la, custe o que custar. Amém.

Day 1: A holy life in the world

Dia 1: Vida santa no mundo

As a young man Thomas More lived for a time near the Carthusians and seriously weighed a monastic or priestly vocation, but discerned that God was calling him to serve as a layman, husband, and public servant. He carried a hidden discipline of prayer into a worldly life. He teaches us that the path to holiness runs straight through the ordinary duties of family, work, and daily station.

Quando jovem, Tomás Moro viveu por um tempo junto aos cartuxos e ponderou seriamente uma vocação monástica ou sacerdotal, mas discerniu que Deus o chamava a servir como leigo, esposo e servidor público. Levou uma oculta disciplina de oração para uma vida no mundo. Ele nos ensina que o caminho da santidade passa reto pelos deveres comuns da família, do trabalho e do estado de cada dia.

Prayer

O St. Thomas More, who found holiness amid the cares of family and public life, help me to seek God faithfully in the duties of my own state, making of my daily work and home a path that leads me to Him. Amen.

Ó São Tomás Moro, que encontrastes a santidade em meio aos cuidados da família e da vida pública, ajudai-me a buscar a Deus com fidelidade nos deveres do meu próprio estado, fazendo do meu trabalho e do meu lar diários um caminho que me conduza a Ele. Amém.

Intention

For the grace to seek holiness in my daily state of life.

Pela graça de buscar a santidade no meu estado de vida cotidiano.

Day 2: A man of prayer

Dia 2: Homem de oração

Amid the heavy burdens of high office, More rose long before dawn for prayer, Scripture, and Mass, and wore a rough hair shirt hidden beneath his fine chancellor's robes. Outwardly busy and brilliant, inwardly he lived face to face with God. He shows us that the most demanding life must be rooted in daily, hidden union with the Lord, or it will lose its soul.

Em meio aos pesados encargos de um alto cargo, Moro levantava-se muito antes do amanhecer para a oração, a Escritura e a Missa, e usava um áspero cilício oculto sob as finas vestes de chanceler. Por fora ocupado e brilhante, por dentro vivia face a face com Deus. Ele nos mostra que a vida mais exigente deve enraizar-se na união diária e oculta com o Senhor, ou perderá a sua alma.

Prayer

O St. Thomas More, who rooted a busy life in hidden prayer, teach me to give God the first hours and the first place each day, that amid all my labors my heart may rest always quietly in Him. Amen.

Ó São Tomás Moro, que enraizastes uma vida atarefada na oração oculta, ensinai-me a dar a Deus as primeiras horas e o primeiro lugar cada dia, para que, em meio a todos os meus afazeres, o meu coração repouse sempre tranquilo Nele. Amém.

Intention

For fidelity to daily prayer amid a busy life.

Pela fidelidade à oração diária em meio à vida atarefada.

Day 3: Devoted husband and father

Dia 3: Esposo e pai dedicado

More made his home at Chelsea a joyful school of learning and faith, giving his daughters the same fine education as his son — rare in that age — and gathering all his household for daily prayer. His love was warm, playful, and devout. He teaches us that the love of God begins and is tested in the patient, joyful, everyday love of one's own family.

Moro fez do seu lar em Chelsea uma alegre escola de cultura e de fé, dando às filhas a mesma esmerada educação que ao filho — raro naquele tempo — e reunindo toda a casa para a oração diária. Seu amor era caloroso, brincalhão e devoto. Ele nos ensina que o amor a Deus começa e se prova no amor paciente, alegre e cotidiano da própria família.

Prayer

O St. Thomas More, loving husband and father who raised your household in faith, bless all Christian families, and help parents to teach their children the love of God by word and by joyful example. Amen.

Ó São Tomás Moro, esposo e pai amoroso que educastes a vossa casa na fé, abençoai todas as famílias cristãs, e ajudai os pais a ensinar aos filhos o amor de Deus pela palavra e pelo alegre exemplo. Amém.

Intention

For Christian families and the education of children in the faith.

Pelas famílias cristãs e pela educação dos filhos na fé.

Day 4: Integrity in public office

Dia 4: Integridade no cargo público

As Lord Chancellor of England, More judged swiftly and justly, clearing the backlog of the courts and famously refusing the bribes and favors that corrupted so many around him. He held power as a servant, not a master. He shows us that authority is a sacred trust from God, to be exercised with honesty, diligence, and the fear of the Lord, for the good of all.

Como Lorde Chanceler da Inglaterra, Moro julgava com rapidez e justiça, liquidando o acúmulo de processos dos tribunais e recusando notoriamente os subornos e favores que corrompiam tantos à sua volta. Exercia o poder como servo, não como senhor. Ele nos mostra que a autoridade é um sagrado encargo confiado por Deus, a ser exercido com honestidade, diligência e temor do Senhor, para o bem de todos.

Prayer

O St. Thomas More, just judge who served the common good without corruption, obtain integrity and the fear of God for all who hold public office, that they may use their authority honestly for the good of those entrusted to them. Amen.

Ó São Tomás Moro, justo juiz que servistes o bem comum sem corrupção, alcançai integridade e temor de Deus a todos os que exercem cargos públicos, para que usem a sua autoridade com honestidade pelo bem daqueles que lhes são confiados. Amém.

Intention

For honesty and justice among those who hold public office.

Pela honestidade e justiça entre os que exercem cargos públicos.

Day 5: The voice of conscience

Dia 5: A voz da consciência

When the king's men demanded he swear the Oath of Supremacy against the truth, More chose first silence and then, when pressed, clear refusal, unwilling to damn his soul to please an earthly king. He would not bend his conscience even to save his life. He teaches us that a conscience formed by God's truth must never be sold, however great the threat or the cost.

Quando os homens do rei exigiram que jurasse o Ato de Supremacia contra a verdade, Moro escolheu primeiro o silêncio e depois, pressionado, a clara recusa, não querendo condenar a alma para agradar a um rei terreno. Não dobraria a consciência nem para salvar a própria vida. Ele nos ensina que uma consciência formada pela verdade de Deus jamais deve ser vendida, por maior que seja a ameaça ou o preço.

Prayer

O St. Thomas More, who would not betray your conscience even to save your life, give me the courage to hold fast to God's truth under every pressure, and to obey Him rather than men whenever the two demand opposite things. Amen.

Ó São Tomás Moro, que não traístes a vossa consciência nem para salvar a vida, dai-me a coragem de me apegar firmemente à verdade de Deus sob toda pressão, e de obedecer a Ele mais do que aos homens sempre que os dois exigirem coisas opostas. Amém.

Intention

For the courage to follow my conscience faithfully.

Pela coragem de seguir fielmente a minha consciência.

Day 6: Loss of all things

Dia 6: A perda de todas as coisas

Rather than betray the Church, More gave up his great office, his income, and at last his liberty, leaving his beloved family in real poverty and want. He counted all these as nothing beside his fidelity to God. He shows us that no honor, wealth, or comfort is worth purchasing at the price of faith, and that Christ asks us to set Him above all we hold dear.

Antes de trair a Igreja, Moro abriu mão do seu grande cargo, da sua renda e, por fim, da sua liberdade, deixando a amada família em real pobreza e necessidade. Tinha tudo isso por nada diante da sua fidelidade a Deus. Ele nos mostra que nenhuma honra, riqueza ou conforto vale ser comprado ao preço da fé, e que Cristo nos pede que O ponhamos acima de tudo o que prezamos.

Prayer

O St. Thomas More, who lost all things rather than lose your faith, free my heart from attachment to honor, security, and the esteem of others, that I may hold nothing dearer than fidelity to God. Amen.

Ó São Tomás Moro, que perdestes todas as coisas antes de perder a fé, libertai o meu coração do apego à honra, à segurança e à estima dos outros, para que eu nada tenha por mais precioso do que a fidelidade a Deus. Amém.

Intention

For detachment from honor, security, and human respect.

Pelo desapego da honra, da segurança e do respeito humano.

Day 7: Patience in prison

Dia 7: Paciência na prisão

During fifteen months in the Tower of London, stripped of books and comforts, More wrote tender letters to his daughter Margaret and composed treatises on Christ's Passion and a Dialogue of Comfort against Tribulation, finding peace in his Lord's own suffering. He reminds us that even a prison cell can become a place of consolation and joy when our trials are quietly offered to God.

Durante quinze meses na Torre de Londres, privado de livros e confortos, Moro escreveu cartas ternas à sua filha Margaret e compôs tratados sobre a Paixão de Cristo e um Diálogo do Conforto contra a Tribulação, encontrando paz no próprio sofrimento do seu Senhor. Ele nos recorda que até uma cela de prisão pode tornar-se lugar de consolo e alegria quando as nossas provações são oferecidas em silêncio a Deus.

Prayer

O St. Thomas More, who found peace in prison by uniting your trial to Christ's Passion, comfort all who are imprisoned or persecuted for the faith, and teach me to meet my own hardships with patience and quiet hope. Amen.

Ó São Tomás Moro, que encontrastes a paz na prisão unindo a vossa provação à Paixão de Cristo, consolai todos os que estão presos ou perseguidos por causa da fé, e ensinai-me a enfrentar as minhas próprias dificuldades com paciência e serena esperança. Amém.

Intention

For all who are imprisoned or persecuted for the faith.

Por todos os que estão presos ou perseguidos por causa da fé.

Day 8: Humor on the scaffold

Dia 8: Bom humor no cadafalso

Climbing the unsteady scaffold to his death, More jested with the officer, asking help to go up and promising to see to his own coming down, and bidding the executioner take courage. His wit never left him, for it sprang from a heart utterly at peace with God. He teaches us that a soul truly anchored in the Lord can meet even death with serenity and good cheer.

Subindo o cadafalso instável rumo à morte, Moro brincou com o oficial, pedindo ajuda para subir e prometendo cuidar sozinho da descida, e exortando o carrasco a ter ânimo. Seu bom humor nunca o deixou, pois brotava de um coração inteiramente em paz com Deus. Ele nos ensina que uma alma verdadeiramente ancorada no Senhor pode enfrentar até a morte com serenidade e bom ânimo.

Prayer

O St. Thomas More, who met death with serene good humor born of trust in God, give me a heart so anchored in the Lord that I may face suffering and even death itself with peace, courage, and quiet joy. Amen.

Ó São Tomás Moro, que enfrentastes a morte com sereno bom humor nascido da confiança em Deus, dai-me um coração tão ancorado no Senhor que eu possa enfrentar o sofrimento e a própria morte com paz, coragem e serena alegria. Amém.

Intention

For peace and trust in the face of suffering and death.

Pela paz e confiança diante do sofrimento e da morte.

Day 9: The King's good servant, but God's first

Dia 9: Bom servo do rei, mas primeiro de Deus

At the block More declared himself 'the King's good servant, but God's first,' then forgave his executioner, kissed him, and died in calm faith, his eyes lifted to heaven. He had given his life rather than his conscience. He assures us that fidelity to God, though it should cost us everything in this world, is never truly defeated but is crowned in the next.

No cepo, Moro declarou-se 'o bom servo do rei, mas primeiro de Deus', depois perdoou o carrasco, beijou-o e morreu em serena fé, os olhos erguidos ao céu. Dera a vida, e não a consciência. Ele nos assegura que a fidelidade a Deus, ainda que nos custe tudo neste mundo, nunca é verdadeiramente derrotada, mas é coroada no outro.

Prayer

O glorious St. Thomas More, faithful unto death and the King's good servant but God's first, present to God the petition I now entrust to you, and obtain for me steadfast fidelity and the grace I most desire. Amen.

Ó glorioso São Tomás Moro, fiel até a morte e bom servo do rei, mas primeiro de Deus, apresentai a Deus a súplica que agora vos confio, e alcançai-me firme fidelidade e a graça que mais desejo. Amém.

Intention

For the particular grace I now entrust to St. Thomas More.

Pela graça particular que agora confio a São Tomás Moro.

Closing prayer

O St. Thomas More, good servant of your king but first of God, present my petitions to the Lord for whom you laid down your life. Teach me to serve faithfully in the world without surrendering my soul, until I share your joy in heaven. Amen.

Ó São Tomás Moro, bom servo do vosso rei, mas primeiro de Deus, apresentai os meus pedidos ao Senhor por quem destes a vida. Ensinai-me a servir fielmente no mundo sem entregar a minha alma, até partilhar convosco a alegria do céu. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="thomasmore">
Novena to St.
Thomas More

Say once a day for nine days, especially starting on 30
June and ending on 8 July, the eve of the feast of St. Thomas More

First Day:

Dear St. Thomas More, in your earthly life, you were a model of
prudence. You never thrust yourself rashly into any serious
undertaking; instead, you tested the strength of your powers and waited
on God's will in prayer and penance, then boldly carried it out without
hesitation. Through your prayers and intercession, obtain for me the
virtues of patience, prudence, wisdom and courage.

Recite one Our Father, one Hail Mary, and one Gloria

Glorious St. Thomas More, I beg you to take up my cause, confident that
you will advocate for me before God's Throne with the same zeal and
diligence that marked your career on earth. If it be in accord with
God's will, obtain for me the favor I seek, namely (mention your
intentions here) .

V. Pray for us, O Blessed St. Thomas More.

R. That we may faithfully follow you on the hard road that leads to the
narrow gate of eternal life.

Second Day:

Dear St. Thomas More, in your earthly life, you were a model of
diligence. You shunned procrastination, applied yourself with fervor to
your studies, and spared no effort in achieving mastery in any skill.
Through your prayers and intercession, obtain for me the virtue of
diligence and persistence in my preparations for all undertakings.

Recite one Our Father, one Hail Mary, and one Gloria

Glorious St. Thomas More, I beg you to take up my cause, confident that
you will advocate for me before God's Throne with the same zeal and
diligence that marked your career on earth. If it be in accord with
God's will, obtain for me the favor I seek, namely (mention your
intentions here) .

V. Pray for us, O Blessed St. Thomas More.

R. That we may faithfully follow you on the hard road that leads to the
narrow gate of eternal life.

Third Day:

Dear St. Thomas More, in your earthly life, you were a model of
industriousness. You threw yourself wholeheartedly into everything you
did, and you found enjoyment even in the most serious things. Through
your prayers and intercession, obtain for me the grace of always having
suitable employment, the grace to find interest in everything fitting,
and the fortitude always to pursue excellence in whatever task God
gives me to do.

Recite one Our Father, one Hail Mary, and one Gloria

Glorious St. Thomas More, I beg you to take up my cause, confident that
you will advocate for me before God's Throne with the same zeal and
diligence that marked your career on earth. If it be in accord with
God's will, obtain for me the favor I seek, namely (mention your
intentions here) .

V. Pray for us, O Blessed St. Thomas More.

R. That we may faithfully follow you on the hard road that leads to the
narrow gate of eternal life.

Fourth Day:

Dear St. Thomas More, in your earthly life, you were a brilliant lawyer
and a just and compassionate judge. You attended to the smallest
details of your legal duties with the greatest care, and you were
unflagging in your pursuit of justice tempered by mercy. Through your
prayers and intercession, obtain for me the grace to overcome every
temptation to laxity, arrogance, and rash judgment in my duties.
Recite one Our Father, one Hail Mary, and one Gloria
Glorious St. Thomas More, I beg you to take up my cause, confident that
you will advocate for me before God's Throne with the same zeal and
diligence that marked your career on earth. If it be in accord with
God's will, obtain for me the favor I seek, namely (mention your
intentions here).
V. Pray for us, O Blessed St. Thomas More.

R. That we may faithfully follow you on the hard road that leads to the
narrow gate of eternal life.

Fifth Day:

Dear St. Thomas More, in your earthly life, you were a model of
humility. You never allowed pride to lead you to take on enterprises
beyond your abilities; even in th

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas has a St. Thomas More novena (https://www.praymorenovenas.com/st-thomas-more-novena). Each day opens with a Trinitarian blessing, thanks God for an aspect of More's witness, and adds a petition plus space for personal intentions, closing "through Christ Our Lord. Amen." with a Trinitarian blessing. Daily themes: Day 1 holiness in everyday lay life; Day 2 submission to God's will (his discernment to serve God in the world); Day 3 virtue in professional life (lawyer/Parliament, resisting corruption); Day 4 holiness in family life; Day 5 integrity and honesty; Day 6 the sanctity of marriage (refusing the king's unlawful marriage); Day 7 fortitude; Day 8 courage and commitment to the Church / papal authority; Day 9 joy in suffering (his humor before execution).

Fidelity note

Ember's prayers are an original composition with no shared wording, yet they parallel the PrayMoreNovenas novena almost theme-for-theme: lay holiness, prayer/discernment, family life, integrity in office, conscience, fortitude/loss, fidelity to the Church, and joy/humor in death. Ember's Day 5–6 ("the voice of conscience," "loss of all things") and Day 8 ("humor on the scaffold") closely echo the source's conscience, sacrifice, and "joy in suffering" days. The biography (hair shirt, Tower imprisonment, "the king's good servant but God's first") is accurate. A faithful, closely parallel original; the FishEaters version could not be verified.

St. John the Baptist st-john-baptist-novena

Jun 15–23
description & history

Nine days with the Forerunner of Christ, who pointed to the Lamb of God and gave his life for the truth.

Nove dias com o Precursor de Cristo, que apontou para o Cordeiro de Deus e deu a vida pela verdade.

St. John the Baptist was the cousin and forerunner of Jesus, leaping in his mother's womb at the Visitation and later preaching repentance along the Jordan. He baptized the Lord, declared 'Behold the Lamb of God,' and was beheaded by Herod for condemning an unlawful marriage. This novena draws on his courage, humility, and burning zeal for God.

São João Batista foi primo e precursor de Jesus, exultando no ventre de sua mãe na Visitação e mais tarde pregando a penitência junto ao Jordão. Batizou o Senhor, proclamou 'Eis o Cordeiro de Deus' e foi decapitado por Herodes por condenar um casamento ilícito. Esta novena se inspira em sua coragem, humildade e ardente zelo por Deus.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Glorious St. John the Baptist, voice crying in the wilderness, you prepared the way of the Lord with fearless preaching and a pure heart. As I begin this novena, obtain for me a spirit of repentance and a love of the truth. Help me to point others always to Christ. Amen.

Glorioso São João Batista, voz que clama no deserto, vós preparastes o caminho do Senhor com pregação destemida e coração puro. Ao iniciar esta novena, alcançai-me um espírito de penitência e o amor da verdade. Ajudai-me a apontar sempre os outros para Cristo. Amém.

Day 1: The voice in the wilderness

Dia 1: A voz no deserto

St. John went out into the desert, clothed in camel's hair and eating locusts and wild honey, that nothing might dull his hearing of God. Far from the noise of Jerusalem, the word of God came to him in solitude, as it had come to the prophets. He teaches us that the soul must first be emptied of distraction and comfort before it can become a clear voice for God's word.

São João retirou-se para o deserto, vestido de pelos de camelo e alimentando-se de gafanhotos e mel silvestre, para que nada embotasse sua escuta de Deus. Longe do ruído de Jerusalém, a palavra de Deus veio a ele na solidão, como viera aos profetas. Ele nos ensina que a alma precisa primeiro esvaziar-se da distração e do conforto para tornar-se uma voz límpida da palavra de Deus.

Prayer

O glorious Saint John the Baptist, voice crying in the wilderness, who sought God in silence and solitude, obtain for me a heart freed from the noise and comfort of the world, that I may hear the Lord clearly and become a faithful echo of His word. Amen.

Ó glorioso São João Batista, voz que clama no deserto, que buscaste a Deus no silêncio e na solidão, alcançai-me um coração livre do ruído e do conforto do mundo, para que eu ouça o Senhor com clareza e me torne fiel eco de sua palavra. Amém.

Intention

For the grace of silence and solitude in which to hear God's voice.

Pela graça do silêncio e da solidão em que se possa ouvir a voz de Deus.

Day 2: Preparing the way of the Lord

Dia 2: Preparar o caminho do Senhor

Fulfilling the prophecy of Isaiah, John called the people to make straight the paths of the Lord, leveling the high places of pride and filling the valleys of despair. His whole life was spent clearing the road by which the Savior would come, content to be only the herald and never the King. He teaches us to labor humbly so that Christ may find an open way into every heart.

Cumprindo a profecia de Isaías, João chamava o povo a endireitar as veredas do Senhor, abaixando os altos da soberba e enchendo os vales do desânimo. Sua vida inteira foi gasta abrindo o caminho por onde viria o Salvador, contente em ser apenas o arauto e nunca o Rei. Ele nos ensina a trabalhar humildemente para que Cristo encontre caminho aberto em cada coração.

Prayer

O glorious Saint John the Baptist, herald who prepared the way of the Lord, obtain for me the grace to level every height of pride and to fill the valleys of my discouragement, that Christ may enter freely and reign within my heart. Amen.

Ó glorioso São João Batista, arauto que preparaste o caminho do Senhor, alcançai-me a graça de abaixar todo monte de soberba e de encher os vales de meu desânimo, para que Cristo entre livremente e reine em meu coração. Amém.

Intention

That I may remove from my heart whatever blocks the coming of Christ.

Para que eu remova de meu coração tudo o que impede a vinda de Cristo.

Day 3: A call to repentance

Dia 3: O chamado à penitência

John baptized in the Jordan, urging hearts to turn from sin and bear worthy fruit, warning that the axe is laid to the root of the trees. He did not flatter, but called even soldiers and tax collectors to justice and mercy. He reminds us that conversion is not a single moment but a daily turning back to God, proven by a changed life and not by words alone.

João batizava no Jordão, exortando os corações a abandonarem o pecado e a darem frutos dignos, advertindo que o machado já está posto à raiz das árvores. Não adulava, mas chamava até soldados e cobradores à justiça e à misericórdia. Ele nos recorda que a conversão não é um só instante, mas um retorno diário a Deus, provado por uma vida transformada e não apenas por palavras.

Prayer

O glorious Saint John the Baptist, preacher of repentance, who called sinners to bear worthy fruit, obtain for me true sorrow for my sins and a firm purpose of amendment, that my whole life may show the conversion my lips profess. Amen.

Ó glorioso São João Batista, pregador da penitência, que chamaste os pecadores a darem frutos dignos, alcançai-me uma verdadeira dor por meus pecados e um firme propósito de emenda, para que toda a minha vida revele a conversão que meus lábios professam. Amém.

Intention

For true contrition and a steadfast purpose of amendment.

Por uma contrição verdadeira e um firme propósito de emenda.

Day 4: Behold the Lamb of God

Dia 4: Eis o Cordeiro de Deus

Seeing Jesus approach, John cried out, 'Behold the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world.' With these words he pointed his own disciples away from himself and toward Christ, and they followed the Lord. His joy was to disappear behind the One he announced. He teaches us to point others always to Jesus and to find our gladness in His increase, not our own.

Vendo Jesus aproximar-se, João exclamou: 'Eis o Cordeiro de Deus, que tira o pecado do mundo.' Com essas palavras, afastou de si os próprios discípulos e os apontou para Cristo, e eles seguiram o Senhor. Sua alegria era desaparecer atrás d'Aquele que anunciava. Ele nos ensina a apontar sempre os outros para Jesus e a encontrar nossa alegria no crescimento d'Ele, e não no nosso.

Prayer

O glorious Saint John the Baptist, who pointed to Jesus as the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world, obtain for me eyes of faith to recognize Christ present in the Eucharist and in the poor, and the grace to lead others to Him. Amen.

Ó glorioso São João Batista, que apontaste Jesus como o Cordeiro de Deus que tira o pecado do mundo, alcançai-me olhos de fé para reconhecer Cristo presente na Eucaristia e nos pobres, e a graça de conduzir outros a Ele. Amém.

Intention

That I may recognize Christ present in the Eucharist and in the poor.

Para que eu reconheça Cristo presente na Eucaristia e nos pobres.

Day 5: He must increase, I must decrease

Dia 5: Importa que Ele cresça e eu diminua

When his own disciples grew jealous that the crowds now went to Jesus, John answered serenely, 'He must increase, but I must decrease.' He called himself the friend of the Bridegroom, rejoicing to hear His voice. He shows us the freedom of the soul that desires only God's glory and not its own, content to grow smaller as Christ grows greater in our lives and in the world.

Quando seus próprios discípulos se inquietaram porque as multidões agora iam a Jesus, João respondeu sereno: 'Importa que Ele cresça e eu diminua.' Chamou-se o amigo do Esposo, que se alegra ao ouvir-lhe a voz. Ele nos mostra a liberdade da alma que deseja apenas a glória de Deus e não a própria, contente em diminuir à medida que Cristo cresce em nossa vida e no mundo.

Prayer

O glorious Saint John the Baptist, friend of the Bridegroom, who rejoiced to decrease that Christ might increase, obtain for me deliverance from vanity and the craving to be seen, that I may seek God's glory alone and rejoice as He grows in my soul. Amen.

Ó glorioso São João Batista, amigo do Esposo, que te alegraste em diminuir para que Cristo crescesse, alcançai-me a libertação da vaidade e do desejo de aparecer, para que eu busque somente a glória de Deus e me alegre quando Ele cresce em minha alma. Amém.

Intention

For deliverance from vanity and the desire to be seen.

Pela libertação da vaidade e do desejo de aparecer.

Day 6: Humility before Christ

Dia 6: Humildade diante de Cristo

Though crowds came to him as a prophet and some wondered whether he was the Messiah, John confessed that he was unworthy even to loosen the strap of the Lord's sandal. He declared plainly that he was not the Christ but only a voice. True greatness, he teaches, does not grasp at honor but knows its own place before God, decreasing so that the truth may shine more brightly.

Embora as multidões viessem a ele como a um profeta e alguns se perguntassem se seria o Messias, João confessou-se indigno até de desatar a correia da sandália do Senhor. Declarou claramente que não era o Cristo, mas apenas uma voz. A verdadeira grandeza, ele ensina, não se agarra à honra, mas conhece o próprio lugar diante de Deus, diminuindo para que a verdade resplandeça mais.

Prayer

O glorious Saint John the Baptist, who counted yourself unworthy to loosen the Lord's sandal, obtain for me a humble heart that gladly takes the lowest place, never grasping at honor, but content to be small that God alone may be glorified. Amen.

Ó glorioso São João Batista, que te consideraste indigno de desatar a sandália do Senhor, alcançai-me um coração humilde que de bom grado tome o último lugar, jamais cobiçando a honra, mas contente em ser pequeno para que só Deus seja glorificado. Amém.

Intention

For a humble heart that takes the lowest place gladly.

Por um coração humilde que de bom grado tome o último lugar.

Day 7: Courage to speak the truth

Dia 7: Coragem para dizer a verdade

John did not flatter the powerful, but told Herod plainly that it was unlawful for him to take his brother's wife. He knew this honesty might cost him his freedom and his life, yet he would not soften the truth to win favor. He teaches us that genuine charity sometimes requires the courage to wound in order to heal, and that fidelity to God outweighs the fear of men.

João não adulou os poderosos, mas disse a Herodes claramente que não lhe era lícito tomar a mulher de seu irmão. Sabia que essa franqueza poderia custar-lhe a liberdade e a vida, mas não suavizaria a verdade para ganhar favor. Ele nos ensina que a verdadeira caridade às vezes exige a coragem de ferir para curar, e que a fidelidade a Deus pesa mais que o temor dos homens.

Prayer

O glorious Saint John the Baptist, who fearlessly rebuked the sin of a king, obtain for me the courage to speak the truth with charity and without fear, that I may never betray my conscience to win human favor, but stand firm for what is right. Amen.

Ó glorioso São João Batista, que repreendeste sem temor o pecado de um rei, alcançai-me a coragem de dizer a verdade com caridade e sem medo, para que eu jamais traia minha consciência buscando o favor dos homens, mas permaneça firme no que é justo. Amém.

Intention

For the courage to speak the truth with charity and without fear.

Pela coragem de dizer a verdade com caridade e sem temor.

Day 8: Faithful in prison

Dia 8: Fiel na prisão

Cast into Herod's dungeon for his fidelity, John sent his disciples to ask Jesus, 'Are you the one who is to come?' and waited in the darkness without bitterness. Even in chains he kept turning his followers toward the Lord. He teaches us to hold fast to faith and to keep seeking Christ when God's purposes seem hidden, trusting that the Lord is at work even in our darkest hours.

Lançado no cárcere de Herodes por sua fidelidade, João enviou seus discípulos a perguntar a Jesus: 'És tu aquele que há de vir?' e esperou nas trevas sem amargura. Mesmo acorrentado, continuava a voltar os seus seguidores para o Senhor. Ele nos ensina a permanecer firmes na fé e a continuar buscando Cristo quando os desígnios de Deus parecem ocultos, confiando que o Senhor age mesmo em nossas horas mais escuras.

Prayer

O glorious Saint John the Baptist, faithful in the darkness of Herod's prison, obtain for me perseverance in my trials and steadfast trust when God seems silent or distant, that I may wait upon Him without bitterness and never cease to seek His face. Amen.

Ó glorioso São João Batista, fiel nas trevas do cárcere de Herodes, alcançai-me a perseverança em minhas provações e a confiança firme quando Deus parece silencioso ou distante, para que eu O espere sem amargura e jamais cesse de buscar o seu rosto. Amém.

Intention

For perseverance in trials and trust when God seems distant.

Pela perseverança nas provações e pela confiança quando Deus parece distante.

Day 9: Martyr for the truth

Dia 9: Mártir da verdade

John was beheaded at Herod's command for the sake of righteousness, sealing his witness with his blood rather than retract a single word of truth. The Forerunner who had prepared the way before the Lord in life now goes before Him in death, leading the way of the martyrs to the throne of God. He teaches us to be faithful to the very end, that we too may finish our course in fidelity.

João foi decapitado por ordem de Herodes por causa da justiça, selando com o sangue o seu testemunho em vez de retratar uma só palavra da verdade. O Precursor que preparara o caminho diante do Senhor em vida agora O precede na morte, conduzindo o caminho dos mártires até o trono de Deus. Ele nos ensina a ser fiéis até o fim, para que também nós terminemos nossa jornada na fidelidade.

Prayer

O glorious Saint John the Baptist, who shed your blood rather than betray the truth, obtain for me the grace of final perseverance and fidelity unto death, that having followed Christ faithfully on earth, I may rejoice with you forever before His throne. Amen.

Ó glorioso São João Batista, que derramaste o teu sangue em vez de trair a verdade, alcançai-me a graça da perseverança final e da fidelidade até a morte, para que, tendo seguido fielmente a Cristo na terra, eu me regozije convosco para sempre diante de seu trono. Amém.

Intention

For the grace of final perseverance and fidelity unto death.

Pela graça da perseverança final e da fidelidade até a morte.

Closing prayer

St. John the Baptist, who decreased that Christ might increase, teach me to seek not my own glory but His. Strengthen me to bear witness to the truth even at great cost, and to await the Lamb of God with a watchful heart. Intercede for me, that I may share one day in your joy. Amen.

São João Batista, que diminuístes para que Cristo crescesse, ensinai-me a buscar não a minha glória, mas a d'Ele. Fortalecei-me para testemunhar a verdade ainda que a grande custo, e a esperar o Cordeiro de Deus com coração vigilante. Intercedei por mim, para que um dia eu partilhe de vossa alegria. Amém.

FishEaters traditional

name="baptist">
Novena
to St. John the Baptist

Say once a day for 9 days, especially beginning on
June 15 and ending on June 23, the eve of the Feast of St. John.

O glorious St. John the Baptist, of those born of women the greatest of
prophets; thou, though sanctified from thy mothers womb and most
innocent, didst nevertheless will to retire into the desert, there to
practise austerities and penance; obtain for us from our Lord the grace
to be detached, at least in the affections of our hearts, from all
earthly treasure, and to practise Christian mortification with interior
recollection and a spirit of holy prayer.

Pray one Pater, one Ave,
and one Gloria

O most zealous Apostle, who, without working any miracle on others, but
solely by the example of thy life of penance and the power of thy
words, didst draw after thee crowds, in order to prepare them to
worthily receive the Messiah, and listen to his heavenly teaching;
grant that it may be given to us also, by the example of a holy life
and the practice of good works, to lead many souls to God, and, above
all, those who have been enveloped in the darkness of error and
ignorance, and led astray by vice.

Pray one Pater, one Ave,
and one Gloria

Unconquered martyr, who for the honour of God and the salvation of
souls, didst, with heroic constancy, and at the cost of life itself,
with stand the impious Herod, reproving him openly for his bad

and dissolute life ; obtain for us a brave and generous heart, so that,
overcoming all human respect, we may boldly profess our faith, and
follow the teaching of our divine master Jesus Christ.

Pray one Pater, one Ave,
and one Gloria

V. Pray for us, St John the Baptist.

R. That we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.

Let us pray. O God, Who hast made this day worthy of honour in the
nativity of St John, grant to Thy people the grace of spiritual joys,
and direct the minds of all the faithful into the way of eternal
salvation. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.

<a

PrayMoreNovenas modern

PrayMoreNovenas has a Saint John the Baptist novena (https://www.praymorenovenas.com/saint-john-the-baptist-novena), Trinitarian opening, a unique daily meditation and intention, a closing petition ("Lord Jesus, Your cousin John prepared the way for Your coming..."), "St. John the Baptist, pray for us!", and a Trinitarian blessing. Daily themes there lean on the infancy narrative and ministry: Day 1 miraculous conception; Day 2 recognition of Christ in the womb / protecting the unborn; Day 3 his naming and Zechariah's restored speech; Day 4 desert preparation and repentance; Day 5 Jesus's baptism and John's humility; Day 6 "Behold the Lamb of God"; Day 7 the forerunner / humble rejoicing in God's glory; Day 8 repeats Day 7; Day 9 the greatest prophet.

Fidelity note

Ember's text is an original composition, not a copy of either source. It is closest to the FishEaters novena in spirit — both stress the desert/penance, fearless rebuke of Herod, and martyrdom — though the wording is entirely Ember's and does not reproduce FishEaters' archaic "thou/didst" prayers. Against PrayMoreNovenas the structural match is strong (fixed opening + unique daily meditation + intention), but the day themes diverge: PrayMoreNovenas weights the infancy narrative, whereas Ember organizes around John's mission and Gospel sayings ("make straight the paths," "Behold the Lamb of God," "He must increase, I must decrease"). All meditations are scripturally accurate; a faithful, well-grounded original.

SS. Peter and Paul ss-peter-and-paul-novena

Jun 20–28
description & history

Nine days with the two great apostles, Peter the rock and Paul the teacher of the nations, pillars of the Church of Rome.

Nove dias com os dois grandes apóstolos, Pedro a pedra e Paulo o doutor das nações, colunas da Igreja de Roma.

St. Peter, a Galilean fisherman, was named by Christ the rock on which He would build His Church and given the keys of the kingdom. St. Paul, once a persecutor, was struck down on the road to Damascus and became the tireless apostle to the gentiles. Both crowned their labors with martyrdom in Rome and are honored together on June 29.

São Pedro, pescador da Galileia, foi chamado por Cristo de pedra sobre a qual edificaria a sua Igreja, recebendo as chaves do reino. São Paulo, outrora perseguidor, foi prostrado no caminho de Damasco e tornou-se o incansável apóstolo dos gentios. Ambos coroaram seus trabalhos com o martírio em Roma e são honrados juntos no dia 29 de junho.

Ember original · this app

Opening prayer

Holy Apostles Peter and Paul, chosen pillars of Christ's Church, you laid down your lives in Rome for the faith you proclaimed. As I begin this novena, obtain for me a faith as firm as Peter's and a zeal as burning as Paul's. Watch over the Church you founded with your blood. Amen.

Santos Apóstolos Pedro e Paulo, colunas escolhidas da Igreja de Cristo, vós destes a vida em Roma pela fé que anunciastes. Ao iniciar esta novena, alcançai-me uma fé tão firme quanto a de Pedro e um zelo tão ardente quanto o de Paulo. Velai pela Igreja que fundastes com vosso sangue. Amém.

Day 1: Called from the nets

Dia 1: Chamado das redes
